As a professional Freight Re-location Specialist, I have an occasional need for legal services, every few years on average. It was 2008 and Amanda S Gillis of the Seymour Law Group was assigned to me, via a legal referral service. I got a ticket from the California Highway Patrol (CHP) for improper lane usage. This is typical for me. I don’t speed, but I don’t pay enough attention to all the signs either. Amanda, handled my case exactly as I’m used to from any attorney. That is, with as little involvement from me as possible after the initial consultation. I’m results oriented and admittedly quite spoiled in getting my way in legal matters. I’ve always been able to count on the legal professionals.  This works out great for me because I can remain in my favorite positon, a results oriented client. Then, with confidence, I can get on with my day to day chores earning my weekly stipend. However, when I have to get involved with the methods, constantly checking and worrying about the way things are being done, this makes me an unhappy, non-returning customer. Amanda Gillis gives the worry-free results my spoiled personality is used to. For me, she negotiated a perfectly fair solution for all parties involved and after a brief probationary period, I even got back my court fines. So why a review at this late date? Nine years after using her services? One advantage to waiting (procrastinating) this long is, I have a much larger sample size of other legal services to compare with Amanda and the Seymour Law Group since 2008. As it turns out, not all lawyers are that good. At least from the referral service I use[d]. I don’t want to disparage their name, but their initials are TVC. In fact since 2008, my assigned attorneys, thru TVC have lost every single case I had. Albeit there were only a few, but these other attorneys were either unprepared or un-involved. One refused to take my calls when I was following up to see how things turned out. Turns out, it didn’t turn out. I had a call from another attorney that needed me to re-courier paperwork I sent 2 months before. This was the night before a court date. I had to inform him that unfortunately, my Gulfstream G4 was in the shop. Another law professional told me that I didn’t have much of a case because of the judge’s bias. Weak! By coincidently, this was also a lane usage ticket. These are real horror stories in my opinion. I offer these comparisons because even though I used Amanda’s services once, I called her for legal advice on these and other matters. A kind of sounding board in way. She’s always available or, at least returns my calls. Her listening and mentoring over these 9 years gives me a deeper understanding of the legal environment she works in. Combined with my own observations such as, the CHP is being more level-headed and fair than their reputation. However, the judges, not so much, makes for a greater appreciation of her vocation. My observations are anecdotal of course, but I feel I have just enough experience to give this observation.

Lastly, The legally blonde title? I thought she was a brunette before. I never met her in person but I regularly see a couple of her billboards.  Amanda and the Seymour Law Group keeps the bar high and the convictions low.

I am writing this with the kind of emotion that comes from surviving something you should have never had to face. What Annie Davidian did for me and my family goes far beyond “legal work.” She didn’t just handle a case — she saved my life.

For a year and a half, I lived under a nightmare.
I was falsely accused of things that still shake me to my core — rape, being lifted by the neck, being punched, slapped, dragged by the hair. These weren’t small accusations. These were life-ending accusations. The kind that could take away your future, your freedom, your family, your identity… everything.

And yet, there was nothing to support them.
No bruises.
No injuries.
Two medical exams — absolutely nothing.
Two officers — no visible marks at all.
Photos — no evidence of any harm.

But none of that stopped the DA. None of that stopped the fear. None of that stopped the agony of waking up every day wondering if this lie would end the life I’ve worked so hard to build.
What made it worse was the feeling of being completely alone.

My first lawyer didn’t fight for me.
Didn’t believe in me.
Didn’t communicate.
Didn’t defend me the way someone should when your entire existence is on the line.

There were moments where it felt like the system had already decided who I was without even looking at the truth.

I cannot describe the loneliness of that.
The fear.
The disbelief.
The pain of knowing you’re innocent while the world treats you like a criminal.

Then… something changed.

I met Annie Davidian.
From the second I sat across from her, I felt something I hadn’t felt in a long time: hope.
She didn’t rush me.
She didn’t question my truth.
She didn’t look at me like the accusations defined me.
She looked at me like a human being.
She listened.
She believed me.

She took the time — almost three hours — to truly understand everything.
And then she fought.

Month 1:
Annie had only been hired three days before court.
She showed up, stood before the judge, and immediately took control of the case.
For the first time in a long time, I felt protected.

Month 2:
She faced the DA head-on.
She challenged their claims, exposed the truth, and pushed back in a way no one had done for me yet.

Month 3:
I didn’t even have to make the two-hour drive back to Visalia…
because Annie went for me.
And in that courtroom, the DA finally admitted the truth:
They had no case.
Everything was dismissed.
Every accusation fell apart.
My name was cleared.
My life was given back to me.

There are no words strong enough to explain what it feels like to finally breathe after inhaling fear for so long. Annie didn’t just win a legal fight

— she fought for my truth when the world tried to bury it. She protected my family. She restored my future. She gave me back everything that false accusations tried to take away.

I will never forget what she did for me.
Never.

If you are scared… if you are drowning in lies… if you are losing hope…
please believe me when I say this:
Hire Annie Davidian.
She will fight for you.
She will believe you.
She will save you.

Annie, thank you for saving not just my case — but my life, my dignity, and my future.
My family and I will forever be grateful

Josue Montoya

reviews.ownerResponse:
Okay, let's break this down, since you want to take this right to leaving a defamatory review rather than calling me and speaking to me...for the first time.

Your husband hired me to represent your son. He told me that he just wanted a lawyer that would fight and that would be honest with him, good or bad.

You both were told a different version of events vs. what was in evidence. Not talking about reports, which are always wrapped in a pretty bow, I am talking about video, clear enough to see great detail on my work cell, and captured during the day. Once I obtained the evidence, I went to your son, and we had a conversation about it, as I am as transparent as I am before you retain until I close a case.

I then sat down with your husband and another woman who was present, and went over the evidence with him. I told him the truth. Why? Because I don't blow smoke and walk people into convictions for money, that's not right. Your husband told me he understood but that you would not, because your son is a "mama's boy," and you talk to him all of the time and you don't see reality.

In the meantime, you son gets charged with a new case while he is in custody. The DA makes an offer, many years less than what he facing, and tells me that the offer is only being made because it is me and if your son did not want to take it, the DA would go after your son for the maximum amount of time. So I go and speak with your son about it and he says absolutely not. So I go back to the DA and I counter the offer. The DA says no and the DA literally pulls up the evidence on a laptop, which of course I knew about. I made it clear I already knew the first offer was reasonable with the case having so much SOLID evidence (definitely not common) I go back to your son, tell him about the rejection and say look, this is your case, this is your life, I'll take it to trial and I will fight but you need to understand I can only work with the evidence and facts I have, but okay lets go.

Your son gets mad. He messages me on the jail messaging app, calling me many profanities I won't repeat here. No client has ever talked to me like that or disrespected me to this extent and I'm over here signing "no hostage waivers" at some facilities because some of my clients are considered THAT dangerous.

So your son hires a lawyer to take his case to trial and it has been MONTHS since I was off the case, so since you felt the need to be a keyboard warrior, I knew the timing meant things were either getting real or your son was already convicted. I see the case is set for trial soon, so things are probably getting real, but you're clearly still in the dark.

I am sorry that you feel the need to lash out and try and tarnish my reputation online when we have never spoke and you are upset about your son's case. You had my cell phone number. I met you and your husband on a Saturday prior to retaining. You know how accessible I am.

I am sorry that you are panicking and lashing out at me because I kept my word in being transparent with you and your husband PRIOR to any down payment being made for my services. I am a trial lawyer, I feel it is best to go in with SOMETHING to work with (JURORS ARE NOT STUPID), and while I was willing to do that, I did not think it was the best option for your son. With me, he would have been back home in less than one-third of when he will come home right now if he is convicted at trial and the DA successfully requests the maximum amount of time.

Your son gave me consent to speak to you. Did he give it to his new lawyer? Guessing not, as you would have told him to accept the offer I got him, which I know went up after your son fired me.

Lastly, your son is the only client that has fired me. Pretty sure I am not the issue. I wish you all the best, regardless of how you view me now, as you will understand soon enough.

In 2021 I hired this firm cause in August of 2017, Judge Bianco from family court (Visalia) granted my ex wife who id been divorced from since 2014 a 5 year restraining. Judge Bianco labeled them as, Obscene text, that’s why I was in family court, not criminal. The 5 year restraining order caused me to lose my State Department Security Clearance to work at diplomatic outpost, loosing a 6 figure career. Judge Bianco ordered me to a 52 week BATTERS program and to complete 10 INDIVIDUAL counseling sessions, and verbally stated to come back to court once I have that completed. I completed these task in late 2019, but didn’t go back to court till 2021 cause I didn’t wanna go back without representation. We submitted the completed task, they said no, they wanted a letter of confirmation for the 10 individual counseling sessions and the printed out schedule from the VA wasn’t good enough. I did anger management even though it wasn’t court ordered. By this time we got a new Judge, Judge Wolfe. So then I did the 10 additional plus individual counselings and I got the letter of completion from the VA, and then went back to court in 2022, she ordered me to do a court ordered Phycological evaluation. I thought I was protected under hippa cause Wolfe wanted to go through my medical records and I asked Scott Lyons during the proceedings but he would always make a hand gesture as if he was shoeing away a fly and say”just get this done”. Scott Lyons  would always say, “it’s all about the money, your ex’s lawyer get paid by the hour”.  Also I would NOT meet with Scott Lyons until 30 min before the hearing. In 2023, the psych evaluation report stated I should get some custody, but no, they ordered to do more counseling. Scott Lyons never once tried defending me in the court room, I put my faith in him and that’s my fault for not following my intuition. In august of 2024, we went back to court for a review of the restraining order renewal.  I never signed anything or gave my verbal consent, other then agreeing to proceed, I never agreed to a permanent restraining order yet my ex got it. I was deceived for a lack of better term. Charlie, Scott Lyons son said”well they probably went with the severity of the text messages you sent in 2017”, but months before that he said stated” the text messages have been litigated”, lots of contradictory information. Scott wanted to meet up after the hearing, when we should have met before and discuss options. Also, he told my parents and I, that there is noting we can do, that isn’t true at all, I was appalled when I found out threw new firm on what actions we can take. NOT once did I ever get a reason why the “court” made these decisions, Scott never asked for it, you can ask to get it in writing, but that never happened, it was always a mystery and I could NOT ever get a clear answer. Also, every time I’d call the office to ask a question, they always say just email him and he’d get back to you. I printed out all the emails I sent him since 2021, and not once did he ever respond, literally not one. Save your money, you’re better off representing yourself so you can at least ask the judge questions and defend yourself cause he never once stood up for me. My 5 year restraining turned into a permanent, I spent thousands of dollars for only things to become worse. The day I parted ways with Scott Lyons firm, I felt so much stress come off my back. I gave him my faith and was nothing but a yes man, hoping he was gonna have my back, but nope, definitely a learning experience. I’m not upset, and I made that clear when parting ways, everything that I am writing is simply facts. I really wish the court house would just put all of us on lie detectors, would make this all easier, but I nobody wants that. All this cross talk and anecdotal testimony is beyond flawed. I understand what I did was wrong, more then anyone, but I been doing supervised visits since 2017, management  of supervised visits never ever seen anything like this. Scott Lyons said there’s nothing we can, he lied with a smile on his face.

reviews.ownerResponse: We are sorry to hear that this is your opinion, but we strongly disagree with how you characterized our representation. As we explained to you while your case was ongoing, your case was very risky, as we would have to prove that Fresno PD intentionally ran over your father while he was fleeing from the police. The evidence about what actually happened was mixed. If a jury decided the pursuing police officer only acted negligently, or occurred because of the dangerous actions your father took when fleeing from police, the officer would have been protected by qualified immunity and you would have received nothing in compensation. In spite of these difficulties, we were able to secure a substantial settlement from Fresno PD for you, which you agreed to accept because of the substantial risk that a jury could decide the case against us.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Dear Mr. Derogee,

We are sorry to hear that you did not have a good experience with our firm; our goal is to help everyone we can become whole after being injured. While initially we believed we would be able to help you with your case, continued discovery made it clear that your case did not warrant our representation. We notified you accordingly.

As a courtesy, and per our letter withdrawing from representation, we do not attempt to recover any form of compensation, including attorney’s fees, costs, or liens if we do not resolve your case. As such, we waived any and all fees or costs associated with the work we put into your case. Additionally, none of the work we put into your case would detract from your ability to continue pursuing your case. To make sure of this, we turned over any and all documents related to your case to you at the time we withdrew from representation.

If there is anything further we can do for you please reach out to our office.

Sincerely,
Ryan T. Nelson

reviews.text: My husband​ was involved in a tire explosion accident involving a fuel truck back in 2017, resulting in 2 broken fingers, a dislocated thumb, and a ​laceration to the ​forehead. He had to undergo a surgery involving 4 pins, 2 in his ring finger and 2 in his pinky finger. His laceration to the forehead required 4 staples and now has a bald spot where the scar is. We decided to take legal action against the fuel company because my husband's pinky and ring finger could not fully open or close. He had to change his lifestyle around due to the accident. We hired Clayton Christensen, Associate Attorney at Nelson & Rozier, in late 2017. At our initial meeting with Mr. Christensen, we told him about all the facts regarding the incident and the result of the accident. He decided to take on our case, reassuring us that it was a strong case and he was confident that we would have a positive outcome. Mr. Christensen went above and beyond for my husband's case. After a year and a half of filing paperwork and collecting all the information and evidence, we went to trial. We won the case. Although it was a long process, it was definitely worth the while. Mr. Christensen is a very professional and educated lawyer. He was emotionally invested in my husband's case, which let us know that he truly cared for the well-being of my husband. He always stayed in close communication with us through the entire case, which was reassuring. He goes out of his way to ensure that he has a very strong case. I would recommend Mr. Christensen to anyone that was involved in a personal injury. He is one of the best in Visalia. 10/10.

I went into this asking for a 70/30 custody arrangement. What I learned through the process is that courts generally default to 50/50 custody unless there is something extremely major involved, such as proven drug abuse or serious safety concerns. Regardless of whether you hire a lawyer or not, the outcome in most custody cases will likely be the same. I truly wish I had been made more aware of that upfront instead of being told it was something that could be done. I would have appreciated more transparency about realistic expectations before committing financially.
We had also agreed that I would claim my daughter on taxes every year, but the paperwork for that was never submitted. When tax season came, neither of us was able to properly file for her, which caused unnecessary stress.
Another concern was billing. If you carry a balance, they charge around 20–25% interest monthly, which adds up quickly. In January, I made what I was told would be my final payment. I specifically asked for confirmation that my account was fully paid, and I was told yes. However, about a month and a half later, I received another bill for additional charges. That was frustrating and unexpected.
Additionally, both receptionists were very rude in the beginning. I noticed the behavior early on but continued with the firm. There is a difference between being straightforward and being rude, and many interactions felt passive-aggressive.
As a first-time parent going through custody court, I was extremely nervous and felt like I needed representation. Now that I’ve gone through the process myself, I personally feel it wasn’t worth the cost in my situation. If you are considering hiring a lawyer for child custody, make sure you fully understand realistic outcomes and the billing structure before committing.
